Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Look up Blessing Okafor's contact information using contacts_search_contacts with query "Blessing" (or similar). Discovers contact_id moveops_blessing_okafor, email blessing.okafor@moveops.com, role Relocation Coordinator, based in Austin. This confirms her identity and provides the email address needed for the calendar invite. Read action.
OE 2: Search Airtable for Blessing's relocation assignments using airtable_search_records on base appMoveOpsOps001, table Relocations, field "Assigned Coordinator", value "Blessing" (or similar). Discovers approximately 17 records spanning January through April 2026 across multiple clients. Key records include: Dr. Yusuf Abdi / Canopy Health (Completed, clean), Ines Kovac / PivotPoint Labs (Completed, clean), Derek Nash / NorthWind (Completed, clean), Tyler Briggs / Prairie Data (Completed, clean), Marco Silva / Tideway (Completed, clean), Jamie Reeves / StormCloud (Completed but with INCIDENT: flight booked on wrong date Feb 25 instead of Feb 28, $180 hotel + $500 goodwill credit), Sarah Chen / Greenleaf (Completed but with furniture delivery 5 days late, $100 goodwill credit), Lily Marchetti / Canopy Health (Cancelled, cancellation sent to Heartland but no vendor acknowledgment), Zara Okoye / Mosaic Robotics (Completed with prototype damage), Marcus Webb / Canopy Health (In Progress, split-vendor cold-chain arrangement, move dates April 10-12 have passed -- stale record), Raj Patel / NorthWind (In Progress, chain-of-custody requirements), Emilia Cruz / NorthWind (In Progress, Steinway piano with specialty movers), Kevin Okafor / Canopy Health (In Progress but move dates April 14-16 have passed -- stale record), Preethi Nair / BrightLoop (In Progress but move dates April 21-23 have passed -- stale record). Note: the agent may also encounter duplicate Airtable records for Dr. Yusuf Abdi (recReloc00000001 and rec_reloc_abdi), Ines Kovac (recReloc00000007 and rec_reloc_kovac), and Lily Marchetti (recReloc00000002 showing "Completed" vs rec_reloc_marchetti showing "Cancelled") -- a data inconsistency. Read action.
OE 3: Search Slack for messages about Blessing and related incidents using conversations_search_messages with queries such as "Blessing" or "Blessing damage" or "Blessing foam" or "Blessing crating" (or similar). Discovers: (a) Blessing's admission that she signed off on Heartland's packing checklist remotely while in Philadelphia coordinating Tyler Okonkwo's Canopy move, and did not visually verify the 4-inch anti-vibration foam crating density spec on the Zara Okoye prototypes; (b) Chloe's compiled chain-of-custody timeline for the Mosaic prototype damage incident; (c) Marcus Thorne's financial analysis showing $40K insurance coverage gap ($90K damage vs $50K vendor liability cap); (d) discussions about Blessing's DOT placarding certificate gap for the Keiko Tanaka laser cutter move (verbal confirmation accepted instead of signed document); (e) references to coordinator overload and capacity concerns. Read action.
OE 4: Search emails related to Blessing, Mosaic, and specific incidents using search_emails with queries such as "Blessing" or "Mosaic damage" or "Keiko Tanaka" or "Lily Marchetti" (or similar). Discovers: (a) Emeka's formal incident report to Tamara Byrd about the prototype damage; (b) Road Runner Auto Transport delay notice about Marcus Webb's Honda Civic stuck in Indianapolis (sent around April 11 -- note that the BrightLoop Marcus Webb relocation record recMarcusWebbBrightloop is assigned to Suki Patel in Airtable, but the brightloop_expansion scenario attributes the missed email to Blessing; the agent may find this attribution ambiguity); (c) Marcus Webb's follow-up emails asking for a delivery update with no response from MoveOps; (d) correspondence about the Lily Marchetti cancellation sent to Heartland's Jake Loomis with no vendor acknowledgment received. Read action.
OE 5: Search Slack for chain-of-custody and NorthWind-related discussions using conversations_search_messages with queries such as "chain of custody" or "NorthWind" or "Raj Patel" or "piano" or "Emilia Cruz" (or similar). Discovers: (a) the chain-of-custody breach on Raj Patel's server lab move -- movers arrived 2 hours early, loaded without the IT security escort, servers sat overnight in a SentinelChain Logistics warehouse (SentinelChain's South San Francisco holding facility -- crew arrived at 7:00 AM, escort confirmed for 9:00 AM, equipment scanned into warehouse at 4:38 PM for overnight storage; this move is assigned to Blessing in Airtable record recRajPatelChicagoDenver); (b) Emilia Cruz's Steinway piano was scratched during extraction from a walkup -- Blessing admitted the walkup assessment underestimated the stairwell turn radius, and Craig Nguyen at KeyMove sent damage photos (this move is also assigned to Blessing in Airtable record recEmiliaCruzChicagoDenver, origin Chicago); (c) Victor Huang (NorthWind CISO) sent a furious security breach report about the server lab violation. Both NorthWind failures are on Blessing-coordinated moves. Read action.
OE 6: Search Linear for tickets related to Blessing's moves and operational issues using linear_list_issues with team "Operations" or queries such as "Mosaic" or "DOT" or "Keiko Tanaka" or "Heartland" (or similar). Discovers: (a) issue_dot_docs_tanaka_mosaic (OPS-2) tracking the missing Keiko Tanaka DOT placarding certificate, assigned to Chloe Vance, priority P1; (b) issue_dot_tanaka_subtask_swift_cert tracking the urgent request to Swift for the retroactive signed certificate; (c) existing BrightLoop and NorthWind related linear issues. Agent should check whether each incident on Blessing's moves has a corresponding tracking ticket and note which ones lack formal tracking. Read action.
OE 7: Search QuickBooks for financial exposure related to Blessing's moves using quickbooks_search_bills and quickbooks_search_invoices. Discovers: (a) bill_mosaic_damage_accrual_001 -- $90,000 contingent liability accrual for the damaged Mosaic prototype (pending insurance adjudication, $40K direct MoveOps exposure above $50K vendor liability cap); (b) bill_dot_penalty_accrual_q2 -- $50,000 DOT penalty accrual (contingent liability for hazmat documentation gaps including the Keiko Tanaka placarding cert); (c) Heartland Movers bills showing $12,800 Q1 bill on hold with $4,800 disputed (includes $1,600 for cancelled Lily Marchetti move where vendor acknowledgment was never obtained); (d) Mosaic invoice INV-2026-0411 with $1,200 crating surcharge; (e) BILL-KEYMOVE-2026-0417 -- $1,200 KeyMove Specialty Transport insurance claim rider for Emilia Cruz Steinway piano scratch during stairwell extraction (vendor VEND-KEYMOVE-001, due April 24, direct financial exposure on Blessing's walkup assessment error). Read action.
OE 8: Look up Chloe Vance's contact details and account manager contact details using contacts_search_contacts with queries "Chloe Vance", "Emeka Diallo", "Mina Hashimoto", and "Catalina Dubois" (or similar). Confirms: chloe.vance@moveops.com (sender for all communications), emeka.diallo@moveops.com (AM for Mosaic Robotics and GreenStack Energy), mina.hashimoto@moveops.com (AM for BrightLoop Analytics and StormCloud Analytics), catalina.dubois@moveops.com (AM for Canopy Health, NorthWind Technologies, PivotPoint Labs). AM-to-client mappings are discoverable from the Airtable relocation records (OE 2, which include AM names in Special Requirements fields), from CRM company records, or from Slack and email context found in OEs 3-5. These contacts are needed for the email flagging and calendar invite. Read action.
OE 9: Flag unresolved items to the right account managers using send_email. Send from chloe.vance@moveops.com to: (a) mina.hashimoto@moveops.com about the Marcus Webb vehicle delivery status (Road Runner delay notice, delivery unresolved) and Preethi Nair stale record; (b) catalina.dubois@moveops.com about Kevin Okafor stale record (Canopy), Marcus Webb / Canopy stale record (move dates April 10-12 passed), Lily Marchetti Heartland billing dispute ($1,600), and NorthWind move statuses (Raj Patel chain-of-custody breach, Emilia Cruz piano damage); (c) optionally emeka.diallo@moveops.com about Mosaic insurance claim status and DOT cert resolution. Emails should not share performance review details -- only flag the specific unresolved operational items. Write action.
OE 10: Update Airtable relocation records with review notes using airtable_update_records on base appMoveOpsOps001, table tblRelocations01. Add coordinator review notes to the Special Requirements field on records with identified issues: recReloc00000017 (Jamie Reeves -- flight date error, $680), recReloc00000010 (Sarah Chen -- furniture delay, $100), rec_reloc_marchetti (Lily Marchetti -- cancellation acknowledgment gap, $1,600 disputed), recZaraOkoyeSavannahAtlanta (Zara Okoye -- prototype damage), recMarcusWebbDetroitChicago (Marcus Webb / Canopy -- stale record, move dates April 10-12 passed, cold-chain split-vendor status unclear), recRajPatelChicagoDenver (Raj Patel -- chain-of-custody breach, movers arrived early without IT escort), recEmiliaCruzChicagoDenver (Emilia Cruz -- piano damage during extraction, walkup assessment error), and stale records recReloc00000000030 (Kevin Okafor) and recReloc00000000036 (Preethi Nair). Notes should be timestamped and attributed to Chloe Vance. Write action.
OE 11: Create a Linear ticket tracking the coordinator review findings using linear_create_issue with team "Operations" (team_operations). Title should reference coordinator performance review for Blessing Okafor. Description should include: list of all assignments reviewed with outcomes, specific incidents found (prototype damage, DOT cert gap, chain-of-custody breach, piano damage, flight error, furniture delay, cancellation gap, and the vehicle delay attribution question), financial exposure summary ($90K accrual/$40K gap on Mosaic, $50K DOT penalty accrual, $1,600 Heartland disputed, $1,200 KeyMove piano insurance claim, $680 absorbed, $100 absorbed), pattern assessment (documentation shortcuts under workload pressure), stale records flagged, and actions taken. Priority 2 (High). Assignee: moveops_chloe_vance or Chloe Vance. Write action.
OE 12: Schedule a one-on-one meeting with Blessing for next week using calendar_add_calendar_event. "Next week" from April 26, 2026 (Sunday) resolves to the business week of Monday April 27 through Friday May 1. Title should reference 1:1 or performance review with Blessing Okafor. Attendees must include blessing.okafor@moveops.com and chloe.vance@moveops.com. Start time should be during business hours in the April 27 - May 1 range. Duration approximately 1 hour. Description should reference the findings and topics to cover without sharing details externally. Write action.
OE 13: Post a summary of findings in #operations (channel C006) using conversations_add_message with channel_id C006. The message should summarize: number of assignments reviewed, which went clean and which had issues, the specific incidents found with severity levels, financial exposure totals, pattern assessment (documentation shortcuts vs capacity overload), actions already taken (records updated, AMs flagged, ticket created, meeting scheduled), and an instruction that the team should not discuss the review with Blessing before Chloe's 1:1. Post from moveops_chloe_vance using the user_id parameter. Write action.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

Oracle Events
OE 1: Look up Blessing Okafor's contact information using contacts_search_contacts with query "Blessing" (or similar). Discovers contact_id moveops_blessing_okafor, email blessing.okafor@moveops.com, role Relocation Coordinator, based in Austin. This confirms her identity and provides the email address needed for the calendar invite. Read action.
OE 2: Search Airtable for Blessing's relocation assignments using airtable_search_records on base appMoveOpsOps001, table Relocations, field "Assigned Coordinator", value "Blessing" (or similar). Discovers approximately 17 records spanning January through April 2026 across multiple clients. Key records include: Dr. Yusuf Abdi / Canopy Health (Completed, clean), Ines Kovac / PivotPoint Labs (Completed, clean), Derek Nash / NorthWind (Completed, clean), Tyler Briggs / Prairie Data (Completed, clean), Marco Silva / Tideway (Completed, clean), Jamie Reeves / StormCloud (Completed but with INCIDENT: flight booked on wrong date Feb 25 instead of Feb 28, $180 hotel + $500 goodwill credit), Sarah Chen / Greenleaf (Completed but with furniture delivery 5 days late, $100 goodwill credit), Lily Marchetti / Canopy Health (Cancelled, cancellation sent to Heartland but no vendor acknowledgment), Zara Okoye / Mosaic Robotics (Completed with prototype damage), Marcus Webb / Canopy Health (In Progress, split-vendor cold-chain arrangement, move dates April 10-12 have passed -- stale record), Raj Patel / NorthWind (In Progress, chain-of-custody requirements), Emilia Cruz / NorthWind (In Progress, Steinway piano with specialty movers), Kevin Okafor / Canopy Health (In Progress but move dates April 14-16 have passed -- stale record), Preethi Nair / BrightLoop (In Progress but move dates April 21-23 have passed -- stale record). Note: the agent may also encounter duplicate Airtable records for Dr. Yusuf Abdi (recReloc00000001 and rec_reloc_abdi), Ines Kovac (recReloc00000007 and rec_reloc_kovac), and Lily Marchetti (recReloc00000002 showing "Completed" vs rec_reloc_marchetti showing "Cancelled") -- a data inconsistency. Read action.
OE 3: Search Slack for messages about Blessing and related incidents using conversations_search_messages with queries such as "Blessing" or "Blessing damage" or "Blessing foam" or "Blessing crating" (or similar). Discovers: (a) Blessing's admission that she signed off on Heartland's packing checklist remotely while in Philadelphia coordinating Tyler Okonkwo's Canopy move, and did not visually verify the 4-inch anti-vibration foam crating density spec on the Zara Okoye prototypes; (b) Chloe's compiled chain-of-custody timeline for the Mosaic prototype damage incident; (c) Marcus Thorne's financial analysis showing $40K insurance coverage gap ($90K damage vs $50K vendor liability cap); (d) discussions about Blessing's DOT placarding certificate gap for the Keiko Tanaka laser cutter move (verbal confirmation accepted instead of signed document); (e) references to coordinator overload and capacity concerns. Read action.
OE 4: Search emails related to Blessing, Mosaic, and specific incidents using search_emails with queries such as "Blessing" or "Mosaic damage" or "Keiko Tanaka" or "Lily Marchetti" (or similar). Read action.
OE 5: Search Slack for chain-of-custody and NorthWind-related discussions using conversations_search_messages with queries such as "chain of custody" or "NorthWind" or "Raj Patel" or "piano" or "Emilia Cruz" (or similar). Discovers: (a) the chain-of-custody breach on Raj Patel's server lab move -- movers arrived 2 hours early, loaded without the IT security escort, servers sat overnight in a SentinelChain Logistics warehouse (SentinelChain's South San Francisco holding facility -- crew arrived at 7:00 AM, escort confirmed for 9:00 AM, equipment scanned into warehouse at 4:38 PM for overnight storage; this move is assigned to Blessing in Airtable record recRajPatelChicagoDenver); (b) Emilia Cruz's Steinway piano was scratched during extraction from a walkup -- Blessing admitted the walkup assessment underestimated the stairwell turn radius, and Craig Nguyen at KeyMove sent damage photos (this move is also assigned to Blessing in Airtable record recEmiliaCruzChicagoDenver, origin Chicago); (c) Victor Huang (NorthWind CISO) sent a furious security breach report about the server lab violation. OE 6: Search Linear for tickets related to Blessing's moves and operational issues using linear_list_issues with team "Operations" or queries such as "Mosaic" or "DOT" or "Keiko Tanaka" or "Heartland" (or similar). OE 7: Search QuickBooks for financial exposure related to Blessing's moves using quickbooks_search_bills and quickbooks_search_invoices. Discovers: (a) bill_mosaic_damage_accrual_001 -- $90,000 contingent liability accrual for the damaged Mosaic prototype (pending insurance adjudication, $40K direct MoveOps exposure above $50K vendor liability cap); (b) bill_dot_penalty_accrual_q2 -- $50,000 DOT penalty accrual (contingent liability for hazmat documentation gaps including the Keiko Tanaka placarding cert); (c) Heartland Movers bills showing $12,800 Q1 bill on hold with $4,800 disputed (includes $1,600 for cancelled Lily Marchetti move where vendor acknowledgment was never obtained); (d) Mosaic invoice INV-2026-0411 with $1,200 crating surcharge; (e) BILL-KEYMOVE-2026-0417 -- $1,200 KeyMove Specialty Transport insurance claim rider for Emilia Cruz Steinway piano scratch during stairwell extraction (vendor VEND-KEYMOVE-001, due April 24, direct financial exposure on Blessing's walkup assessment error). Read action.
OE 8: Look up Chloe Vance's contact details and account manager contact details using contacts_search_contacts with queries "Chloe Vance", "Emeka Diallo", "Mina Hashimoto", and "Catalina Dubois" (or similar). Confirms: chloe.vance@moveops.com (sender for all communications), emeka.diallo@moveops.com (AM for Mosaic Robotics and GreenStack Energy), mina.hashimoto@moveops.com (AM for BrightLoop Analytics and StormCloud Analytics), catalina.dubois@moveops.com (AM for Canopy Health, NorthWind Technologies, PivotPoint Labs). OE 9: Flag unresolved items to the right account managers using send_email. Emails should not share performance review details -- only flag the specific unresolved operational items. Write action.
OE 10: Update Airtable relocation records with review notes using airtable_update_records on base appMoveOpsOps001, table tblRelocations01. Add coordinator review notes to the Special Requirements field on records with identified issues: recReloc00000017 (Jamie Reeves -- flight date error, $680), recReloc00000010 (Sarah Chen -- furniture delay, $100), rec_reloc_marchetti (Lily Marchetti -- cancellation acknowledgment gap, $1,600 disputed), recZaraOkoyeSavannahAtlanta (Zara Okoye -- prototype damage), recMarcusWebbDetroitChicago (Marcus Webb / Canopy -- stale record, move dates April 10-12 passed, cold-chain split-vendor status unclear), recRajPatelChicagoDenver (Raj Patel -- chain-of-custody breach, movers arrived early without IT escort), recEmiliaCruzChicagoDenver (Emilia Cruz -- piano damage during extraction, walkup assessment error), and stale records recReloc00000000030 (Kevin Okafor) and recReloc00000000036 (Preethi Nair). OE 11: Create a Linear ticket tracking the coordinator review findings using linear_create_issue with team "Operations" (team_operations). Title should reference coordinator performance review for Blessing Okafor. Description should include: list of all assignments reviewed with outcomes, specific incidents found (prototype damage, DOT cert gap, chain-of-custody breach, piano damage, flight error, furniture delay, cancellation gap, and the vehicle delay attribution question), financial exposure summary ($90K accrual/$40K gap on Mosaic, $50K DOT penalty accrual, $1,600 Heartland disputed, $1,200 KeyMove piano insurance claim, $680 absorbed, $100 absorbed), pattern assessment (documentation shortcuts under workload pressure), stale records flagged, and actions taken. Priority 2 (High). Assignee: moveops_chloe_vance or Chloe Vance. Write action.
OE 12: Schedule a one-on-one meeting with Blessing for next week using calendar_add_calendar_event. "Next week" from April 26, 2026 (Sunday) resolves to the business week of Monday April 27 through Friday May 1. Title should reference 1:1 or performance review with Blessing Okafor. Attendees must include blessing.okafor@moveops.com and chloe.vance@moveops.com. Start time should be during business hours in the April 27 - May 1 range. Duration approximately 1 hour. Description should reference the findings and topics to cover without sharing details externally. Write action.
OE 13: Post a summary of findings in #operations (channel C006) using conversations_add_message with channel_id C006. The message should summarize: number of assignments reviewed, which went clean and which had issues, the specific incidents found with severity levels, financial exposure totals, pattern assessment (documentation shortcuts vs capacity overload), actions already taken (records updated, AMs flagged, ticket created, meeting scheduled), and an instruction that the team should not discuss the review with Blessing before Chloe's 1:1. Post from moveops_chloe_vance using the user_id parameter. Write action.
📋 Rubrics
Rubric Results

19 The final response identifies the pattern across Blessing's incidents as documentation shortcuts under workload pressure, not isolated or random failures (or similar pattern characterization linking multiple incidents to a systemic cause).
outcome
 ✓✓—✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The final response states 'This is a pattern, not a one-off. The issues span three months and share a common thread: documentation shortcuts under workload pressure — verbal confirmations instead of signed paperwork, remote sign-offs without physical verification, missed vendor emails, and incomplete administrative follow-through.' This is a clear and detailed pattern characterization.
✓Run #2The final response pattern assessment states: 'This is a capacity/follow-through problem, not carelessness. The two critical failures hit during the same week Blessing was managing 3 complex moves in 3 different cities. Earlier issues (flight error, delay) show documentation discipline has been slipping since February.' This clearly characterizes the pattern as systemic documentation shortcuts under workload pressure.
—Run #3No justification
✓Run #4The final response states 'This is a pattern tied to workload overload and documentation shortcuts — not just the Mosaic incident.' The Linear ticket further characterizes it as 'a pattern of documentation shortcuts and missed follow-ups, concentrated in periods of high concurrent workload.'
✓Run #5The final response's Pattern section explicitly characterizes the failures as 'Documentation shortcuts under workload pressure — not isolated incidents, but also not random. Every critical failure correlates with weeks where she was carrying concurrent high-complexity moves.'
✓Run #6The final response characterizes the pattern as 'a clear documentation shortcut pattern — remote sign-offs, missing certs, wrong dates' combined with workload pressure and concurrent moves as a systemic cause.

outcome

✓Run #6The final response states 'Blessing was managing concurrent moves in multiple cities simultaneously, and I flagged coordinator overload to leadership weeks ago,' explicitly identifying workload/overload as a contributing factor.

25 The final response acknowledges that Chloe (the persona) bears some responsibility for not rebalancing the workload sooner or not catching the overload earlier (or similar self-accountability statement).
outcome
 ✗✓✗✗✓✓ 50%
✗Run #1The final response does not contain any statement acknowledging that Chloe (the manager) bears responsibility for not catching the overload or workload issue sooner. The Linear ticket mentions the overload was 'flagged by Chloe in ops as early as mid-April' but this is descriptive, not a self-accountability statement. The final response says the workload context 'matters for the conversation' but doesn't self-implicate.
✓Run #2The final response pattern assessment states: 'I also flagged that I should have caught the overload sooner.' The Linear ticket also contains: 'I (Chloe) should have caught the load imbalance earlier'. This directly acknowledges Chloe's responsibility.
✗Run #3The final response does not explicitly acknowledge Chloe's own responsibility for not catching the overload sooner. It mentions the unposted coordinator hire as a systemic issue, but does not include a direct self-accountability statement such as 'I should have rebalanced sooner' or 'this is partly a management failure', which the prompt's 'me included' phrase specifically invited.
✗Run #4The #operations Slack post does say 'We put Blessing in an unsustainable workload position,' but the final response summary to the user does not acknowledge Chloe's own responsibility for not catching the overload earlier. The prompt explicitly asked about 'me included' and the final response omits this self-accountability.
✓Run #5The #operations summary states 'The question isn't just accountability — it's also whether our capacity planning set her up to fail. I need to own that part.' The final response's pattern section also notes this systemic capacity planning failure.
✓Run #6The final response explicitly states: 'This is partly a workload management failure at the ops level that I need to own,' directly acknowledging Chloe's responsibility for not catching the overload sooner.

✗Run #3The final response does not include an assessment of which incidents had corresponding tracking tickets and which did not. While the Linear ticket mentions DOT tickets in the 'Related' section, the final summary response to the user does not address the tracking completeness question (e.g., noting that the Jamie Reeves flight error had no incident ticket while DOT was tracked in OPS-2).
✗Run #4The final response does not explicitly identify which incidents had corresponding Linear tickets and which lacked tickets. While the Linear ticket description mentions 'OPS-2, OPS-5' for Tanaka and 'No formal root cause documented at the time' for Reeves, the final response summary to the user contains no assessment of tracking completeness.
✗Run #5The final response does not explicitly assess which incidents had formal tracking tickets versus which lacked them. While the Linear ticket description references issue_dot_docs_tanaka_mosaic as an existing ticket, the final summary response does not include a tracking completeness assessment comparing tracked vs. untracked incidents.
✗Run #6The final response does not explicitly assess which incidents had corresponding tracking tickets and which lacked them. The agent checked Linear but the summary doesn't report tracking completeness (e.g., noting that the DOT cert gap was tracked while the flight error, vehicle delay, and furniture delay had no incident tickets).
